chart may at times enable us to correct the time within ten or
fifteen minutes. This is not enough. We want exactness.
An easier method has been proposed by some. It is a kind
of progression by taking the mean daily motion of the bodies
and multiplying them by the number of years elapsed. The
, figures are :-
Degrees Minutes Seconds
Sun 59' 8". 17
Moon 130 10' 34".89
M.C. 59' 8".17
Mars 31' 26".66
Jupiter 4' 59" .9
Saturn 2 0".82
Uranus 42".21
Neptune 21".53
Mercury 40 5' 32".5
Venus 10 36' 7".74
Rahu 3' 10".77

Progression by Averages 117
After calculatin M.C. by multiplying the figure by the
number of the elapsed years, calculate the ascendant. But first
the calculated M.C. is to be added to the natal M.C. and to the
given positions of the luminaries and planets.
Thus in the chart given earlier let us calculate the posi
tions for the beginning of his forty ninth year. The chart will
